Feed post

Mr. Maxwell Muthui Mwangi

• 3rd+

🎓Webmaster | D.L.Programmer | EdTech & Instructional Design Leader | Full Stack Software Engineer | AI & Data Science Expert | UI/UX Guru/Pro | Cybersecurity Specialist | Game Developer | IT Entrepreneur | 300+ Certs

6h •

🤖 Will AI replace your job?

It's one of the most debated questions of our time.
But I believe we're asking the wrong question.

The real question is:
👉 What happens when human creativity meets artificial intelligence?

For years, we've been told that AI will automate tasks, eliminate jobs, and fundamentally change how we work.
And while AI is certainly transforming industries, there is something many people overlook:
The future of work isn't Human vs AI.
It's Human + AI.
The organizations that will lead the next decade won't be those that simply adopt AI.

They'll be the ones that learn how to combine:
✅ Human creativity
✅ Strategic thinking
✅ Emotional intelligence
✅ Innovation
with
🚀 Artificial Intelligence
🚀 Automation
🚀 Data-driven decision making
🚀 Intelligent workflows

In my latest article, "The Future of Work: AI, Automation, and Human Creativity," I explore:
🔹 Why AI is more likely to augment jobs than replace them
🔹 The rise of AI-powered workplaces
🔹 Why creativity is becoming more valuable in the age of automation
🔹 How organizations can prepare for the future of work
🔹 The role of unified AI ecosystems like Tattiana AI in transforming productivity

One insight stood out to me:
The more powerful AI becomes, the more valuable uniquely human skills become.
Technology scales capability.
Humans define direction.
If you're a leader, educator, entrepreneur, developer, student, or technology enthusiast, this conversation will shape your future.

📖 Read the full article and share your perspective.
Do you believe AI will replace jobs—or redefine them?
#FutureOfWork #ArtificialIntelligence #AI #Automation #Innovation #Leadership #DigitalTransformation #HumanCreativity #Technology #Productivity #EdTech #SoftwareEngineering #DataScience #TattianaAI #WorkplaceInnovation

Tech Evolution with Ballershop

The Future of Work: AI, Automation, and Human Creativity

Mr. Maxwell Muthui Mwangi

Feed post

Akshat Gupta

• 3rd+

Chartered Accountant | Morgan Stanley | Finance, Automation, AI

22h •

Unpopular opinion: Automation runs laps around AI in increasing a workflow’s efficiency.

Most people treat “AI” and “Automation” alike, but they aren’t. Automation is deterministic. It follows rules. If invoice arrives → extract data → match PO → send for approval. No tokens usage. No data sent to OpenAI.

AI is for the step where a human used to make a judgment call. The exception. The thing that doesn’t follow a fixed pattern.

Here’s what’s actually happening in most “AI implementations” I see: it’s 85% automation with a thin layer of AI on top — and the AI gets all the credit.

Automation has been doing this work since orchestration tools like Zapier launched in 2011. Since Excel macros existed. The plumbing has been there for over a decade.

That’s why my advice to teams is simple:
Automate first. AI second.
Before you spend money on tokens or sign up for the 10th AI tool — audit your workflows first.

How many of your manual processes follow a fixed, repeatable logic?
That’s your automation backlog, not your AI backlog.

3 reactions3

𝐅𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝐌𝐚𝐧𝐮𝐚𝐥 𝐁𝐨𝐭𝐭𝐥𝐞𝐧𝐞𝐜𝐤 𝐭𝐨 𝐀𝐈-𝐏𝐨𝐰𝐞𝐫𝐞𝐝 𝐄𝐟𝐟𝐢𝐜𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐲: 𝐇𝐨𝐰 𝐀𝐠𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐢𝐜 𝐀𝐈 𝐓𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐬𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦𝐞𝐝 𝐋𝐞𝐠𝐚𝐥 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐅𝐢𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐜𝐡 𝐃𝐨𝐜𝐮𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐜𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐠 📄

Many organizations still spend countless hours manually extracting information from documents, creating operational bottlenecks and increasing the risk of errors.

With Agentic AI, document upload and data extraction can be automated while keeping humans in the loop for validation and approval. This approach combines the speed of AI with the reliability of human expertise.

This balance between AI efficiency and human judgment delivered measurable business impact:

✅ 75% reduction in document processing time
✅ 95% data accuracy improvement
✅ Faster operations without compromising trust and compliance

The key takeaway?

The most effective AI solutions are not about replacing people, they are about empowering teams to work smarter, faster, and with greater confidence.

    Ravi Bhojani

    • 3rd+

    Helping Founders & Agencies Build Scalable Web & App Products | Full-Service Dev Agency | Clutch Top Rated | 15+ Years | Web3 · eCommerce · Custom Software

    View my services

    23h •

    The Hidden Cost of AI Automation Failures: Why Ecommerce Brands Are Quietly Rolling Back Their Agents in 2026

    Some ecommerce brands are quietly pulling the plug on their AI agents in 2025. Not announcing it. Just rolling back.

    I spoke with a DTC founder last month who'd automated their entire customer support and reorder workflow using an off-the-shelf AI agent.

    For 6 weeks, it worked beautifully.

    Then it started misrouting refund requests. Customers got wrong order confirmations. One high-value wholesale account nearly walked.

    Estimated damage? $18,000 in chargebacks and one very tense week.

    The US government's suspension of certain AI tools this year isn't just a policy story. It's a signal — reliability and trust in AI systems are real, unsolved problems.

    Most vendors sell you the dream. Few audit what breaks at scale.

    At Alian, we don't just build AI automations. We stress-test them. We map failure scenarios before go-live. We set human checkpoints where the cost of an error is too high to leave to a model.

    The brands winning with AI aren't the boldest experimenters. They're the most deliberate ones.

    If you're evaluating AI automation for your ecommerce ops and want a second opinion before you build — DM me "audit".

    #EcommerceAutomation #AIStrategy #DTC #AIImplementation #AilanSoftware

    4h •

    Every business owner I talk to says the same thing:
    "I don't have enough time."

    But here's the truth nobody tells them —
    The time isn't missing. It's being wasted on work AI should be doing.

    Here's what AI automation actually does for a business:
    ⚡ Responds to leads instantly — no more losing clients because you replied too late
    ⚡ Follows up automatically — your pipeline never goes cold again
    ⚡ Generates reports in seconds — no more manual data pulling every week
    ⚡ Onboards new clients hands-free — welcome emails, docs, check-ins all triggered automatically
    ⚡ Handles support 24/7 — your customers get answers even at 2am

    The businesses winning in 2026 aren't the ones working harder.

    They're the ones who built systems that work while they sleep.

    AI automation isn't the future anymore.

    It's the present — and your competitors are already using it.

    21m •

    AI experiments are yesterday's news for most businesses.

    The groundbreaking phase starts now.

    We're moving from AI tools to AI agents.

    Instead of helping employees write emails or summarize documents, AI agents can now:

    Read incoming emails
    Update CRM records
    Generate reports
    Assign tasks
    Trigger workflows

    The businesses gaining an advantage aren't necessarily using more AI.

    They're building systems where AI can actually take action.

    The question isn't: "Should we use AI?"

    The question is: "Which business process should AI handle first?"

    What repetitive task would you automate in your business today?

      8h •

      One realization changed how I think about AI.

      AI isn't replacing human potential.

      It's amplifying it.

      The most successful people I know aren't using AI to avoid work. They're using it to do higher-value work. They automate repetitive tasks so they can spend more time thinking, creating, building relationships, and solving meaningful problems.

      That's why I believe the future isn't about competing with AI.

      It's about combining human creativity with artificial intelligence.

      When technology handles the routine, humans can focus on what makes us uniquely human.

      And that's where the biggest opportunities will come from.

      #AI #ArtificialIntelligence #Innovation #FutureOfWork #Automation #Leadership #Technology #Productivity

      Gnaneshwar konda

      • 3rd+

      I have experience in: • Business Analysis• Requirement Gathering & Documentation• Agile/Scrum Methodologies• Stakeholder Management• UAT Coordination• Banking & Testing Domain Knowledge• API & Process Analysis

      1h •

      Artificial Intelligence: Transforming the Future of Work

      Artificial Intelligence (AI) is no longer a futuristic concept—it's a reality that is reshaping industries, businesses, and our daily lives. From personalized recommendations on streaming platforms to advanced healthcare diagnostics, AI is driving innovation at an unprecedented pace.

      One of the most significant impacts of AI is in the workplace. Organizations are leveraging AI to automate repetitive tasks, improve decision-making, enhance customer experiences, and increase operational efficiency. This allows employees to focus on higher-value activities that require creativity, critical thinking, and human judgment.

      However, AI is not about replacing humans; it's about augmenting human capabilities. The most successful organizations will be those that combine the power of AI with human expertise to create smarter, faster, and more effective solutions.

      As professionals, it is important to embrace continuous learning and stay updated with emerging technologies. Understanding AI, data analytics, automation, and digital transformation will be key skills for future career growth.

      The AI revolution is already here. The question is not whether AI will impact our work and lives—it is how prepared we are to adapt, learn, and leverage its potential.

      Key Takeaway: AI is not just a technology trend; it is a transformative force that is creating new opportunities, redefining industries, and shaping the future of work.

      #ArtificialIntelligence #AI #Innovation #Technology #DigitalTransformation #FutureOfWork #MachineLearning #Automation #CareerGrowth #Leadership

        • 3rd+

        Apprentice at PwC (Wipro) | 5⭐ HackerRank | 3× Hackathon Winner 🏆 | Google Cloud ML Certified | DSA (Java) | Python, MySQL | Built iters.live | Open to Internships & Projects

        31m •

        After years building AI and automation systems at Wipro and for startups and other teams, I kept running into the same problem: most people don't actually know how to use AI.
        Not which model to pick. Not where it belongs. Not how to automate the right work, or clean up the slow, messy code underneath it.
        People don't need more AI. They need someone who knows exactly where to put it.
        So I built something to fix that. IntegraAI Studio. Here's where I can help:
        🤖 AI integration, done where it actually pays off (not bolted on everywhere)
        ⚙️ Automations that delete the boring, repetitive work and hand your team back hours
        💸 AI cost reduction, trimming bloated LLM and API bills without losing quality
        🌐 Websites and performance, faster load times and green Core Web Vitals
        📊 Data and pipelines that scale instead of breaking
        🧩 Codebase cleanups and rescues nobody else wants to touch
        Not on the list? Tell me anyway. If it's in your stack and it's worth building, we'll build it.
        No agency markup. No vendor lock-in. Just an engineer who's already shipped this.
        (One client came to me with a slow app and crashes every third night at 3am. One weekend later it was faster, the crashes had stopped, and half the code was gone.)
        So, what's the one thing in your stack you've been putting off because it feels too messy to touch?

        We’re Hiring – AI Implementation Project Manager

        We have an exciting opportunity with one of our clients and are looking for a hands-on AI Implementation Project Manager to lead delivery across a portfolio of AI-enabled solutions.
        Location: New York, NY – Remote
        Contract

        The selected individual will drive end-to-end execution (planning, deployment, adoption) and coordinate across business, data, and technology teams, as well as external partners. This role requires proven experience in AI automation programs and strong project management expertise in global, matrixed environments.

        Responsibilities
        Lead delivery of AI-enabled solutions, including:
        Smart intake and agentic orchestration
        Contract intelligence and review automation
        Workflow automation and AI-enabled process transformation
        AI-driven procurement and sourcing capabilities
        Manage end-to-end program delivery: planning, milestones, risks, dependencies, governance.
        Coordinate cross-functional global teams and multiple vendors.
        Drive adoption and change management for AI solutions.
        Provide clear communication and reporting to stakeholders.
        Quickly unblock delivery challenges and ensure execution continuity.

        Required Skills & Experience
        7+ years of experience in AI Agents & Project Management.
        Proven track record of delivering at least one AI automation or digital transformation program end-to-end.
        Strong program delivery capabilities (planning, governance, risk management).
        Familiarity with AI-enabled solutions (intelligent intake, contract intelligence, workflow automation, semantic AI-ready data concepts).
        Experience coordinating cross-functional, global teams and multiple vendors.
        Ability to operate in complex, matrixed environments.
        Hands-on AI implementation experience (not just advisory/strategy).
        Experience Required: 10+ years overall.

        If this role interests you, please share your updated resume along with your contact details and a convenient time to connect.

        21h •

        Want repetitive tasks to disappear without breaking your customer experience?

        I build AI agents that handle lead qualification, customer support, sales follow-up, reporting/analytics, internal workflows, and scheduling assistants. Each agent is tailored to the role it plays in your business.

        They carry conversations, qualify leads, automate decisions, generate insights, and execute repetitive tasks so your team focuses on higher-value work. In practice that means 24/7 responses, faster conversion cycles, fewer manual handoffs, consistent processes, and easier scaling when demand spikes.

        My simple 3-step approach: identify the highest-impact use case, build and train the agent with your data and rules, then optimize it based on real usage and metrics. That keeps deployments practical, measurable, and low-risk.

        DAY 28 of 45
        "Workflows vs Agents"
        Not every automation needs an AI Agent.
        📋 Workflows follow predefined rules.
        🤖 Agents adapt, reason, and make decisions.
        The smartest organizations won't replace workflows with agents, they'll combine both to build intelligent, scalable, and autonomous systems.
        The future of Digital Transformation lies in knowing when to automate and when to empower AI to think.
        Which would create more value in your organization today: a workflow or an AI Agent?

        AI Just Took Another Big Step Forward

        At #WWDC2026, Apple unveiled its new 𝗔𝗜-𝗽𝗼𝘄𝗲𝗿𝗲𝗱 𝗮𝘀𝘀𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗻𝘁, 𝗦𝗶𝗿𝗶 𝗔𝗜, marking the company's biggest #AI move yet. The upgraded assistant can understand context, interact across apps, analyze on-screen content, and provide more natural conversations than previous versions.

        This announcement highlights a larger industry trend:
         • AI is moving beyond chatbots
         • AI is becoming an active digital assistant
         • AI is starting to perform tasks instead of simply answering questions

        We're entering an era where 𝗔𝗜 𝗰𝗮𝗻 𝘀𝗰𝗵𝗲𝗱𝘂𝗹𝗲 𝗮𝗽𝗽𝗼𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀, 𝗾𝘂𝗮𝗹𝗶𝗳𝘆 𝗹𝗲𝗮𝗱𝘀, 𝗺𝗮𝗻𝗮𝗴𝗲 𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸𝗳𝗹𝗼𝘄𝘀, 𝘀𝘂𝗽𝗽𝗼𝗿𝘁 𝗰𝘂𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗺𝗲𝗿𝘀, 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗮𝘂𝘁𝗼𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗲 𝗯𝘂𝘀𝗶𝗻𝗲𝘀𝘀 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀 with minimal human involvement.

        For businesses, the question is no longer "Should we use AI?"

        The real question is:

        "Which business processes should AI handle first?"

        The companies that adopt AI-driven automation today will have a significant advantage in 𝗰𝘂𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗺𝗲𝗿 𝗲𝘅𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲, 𝗲𝗳𝗳𝗶𝗰𝗶𝗲𝗻𝗰𝘆, 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝘀𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗮𝗯𝗶𝗹𝗶𝘁𝘆 tomorrow.

        Ashik A

        • 3rd+

        Srm IST Chennai

        17h • Edited •

        Industrial AI Vision Inspection System for Automotive Manufacturing
        During my internship at Renault Nissan Automotive India Pvt. Ltd., I developed an AI-powered visual inspection system to automate the detection of critical vehicle assembly components such as clips and bolts in real time.
        The system was designed to support manufacturing quality assurance by combining Computer Vision, Edge AI, and Industrial Automation technologies. It performs real-time inspection using multiple camera streams, detects component presence/absence, and communicates inspection results to PLC-controlled production systems.
        Key Features:
        • Real-time object detection and tracking
        • Edge AI deployment on NVIDIA Jetson
        • Multi-camera RTSP stream processing
        • TensorRT-optimized inference
        • PLC integration for automated OK/NG decisions
        • Production-oriented inspection workflow
        This project provided valuable hands-on experience in deploying AI solutions for real-world manufacturing environments and strengthened my knowledge of Computer Vision, Edge AI, and Industrial Automation.

        Feed post

        Gajanan Kulkarni

        • 3rd+

        Agentic AI,Generative AI,LLM, Python,Service Automation,GenAI Apps,Data Engineering, Microsoft Fabric and Analytics,MLOps,LLMOps,ITIL 4,COBIT5, Servicenow GRC,Secops

        3h • Edited •

        Everyone keeps arguing about which AI is smartest — ChatGPT, Gemini, and the rest. Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella just said we're focused on the wrong thing.
        His point is simple: the AI tool isn't what makes a business special. Anyone can buy the same one. What matters is what your team teaches it.

        Think of a kitchen. Any restaurant can buy a great oven. But your grandmother's recipes are yours alone. AI is the oven. Your company's know-how is the recipe.

        The more your people work with AI and shape it around how YOU actually operate, the better it gets at your specific business — until it becomes something no rival can copy. Nadella calls this the "learning loop."

        His warning: if you hand everything over to someone else's AI and stop building this for yourself, you slowly lose what made you valuable. You become replaceable.

        This is exactly the principle we're building AutomationEdge features around — helping companies turn the way they actually work into automation they own, instead of renting their intelligence from a model they don't control.
        Because in the end, you can hand off the work. You can't hand off the learning.
        Don't just use AI. Teach it.
        #AI #Automation #FutureOfWork #Business

        6h •

        🚀 One thing I've learned while building production AI systems:
        Many people think AI applications are just about connecting an LLM and writing a prompt.
        In reality, reliable AI systems require much more than that.

        The most effective solutions combine:
        ✅ Data processing & validation
        ✅ Semantic search & RAG
        ✅ LLM reasoning
        ✅ Business rules & confidence scoring
        ✅ Structured outputs for downstream systems


        The diagram below represents a simplified architecture of how production-grade AI systems typically work.
        From API requests and data processing to retrieval, reasoning, validation, and business outputs, every layer plays a critical role in delivering accurate and trustworthy results.

        Key takeaway:
        AI becomes truly valuable when strong software engineering principles meet intelligent automation.
        Tech Stack I enjoy working with:
        Python | FastAPI | Django | LangChain | OpenAI API | PostgreSQL | RAG | AWS

        21h • Edited •

        🤖 What if AI could tell you exactly which processes to automate — and prove the ROI before you spend a single dollar?
        That's not the future. That's happening right now.
        Here's the shift most enterprises are missing:
        We've spent years asking "how do we automate this?"
        The smarter question is — "what SHOULD we automate, and why?"
        AI-powered process intelligence changes everything:
        🔍 Step 1 — AI scans your entire product & operations landscape
        It maps every workflow, identifies manual touchpoints, and flags where human effort is being wasted at scale.
        📊 Step 2 — Every process gets an Automation Score (0–100)
        Scored on complexity, volume, error rate, and business impact. No more guessing. No more gut-feel decisions.
        🛠️ Step 3 — AI recommends the exact tool — and tells you why
        UiPath for high-volume RPA. Power Automate for low-code integration. ServiceNow for approval workflows. Velocious for S2P. The right tool for the right job — every time.
        💰 Step 4 — You see the projected ROI before you commit
        Cost savings. Hours freed. Payback period. All calculated. All transparent.
        The result?
        ✅ 40% reduction in operational costs
        ✅ 3× return on automation investment
        ✅ 6-month average payback period
        ✅ Teams focused on strategy, not spreadsheets
        This isn't about replacing people — it's about giving your best people back their time.
        The enterprises winning in 2025 aren't the ones automating the most. They're the ones automating the right things.
        👇 Is your organization using AI to decide what to automate — or still relying on gut instinct? Drop your experience in the comments.

        View my portfolio

        1h •

        Everyone is talking about AI tools.

        I think they're missing the bigger shift.

        AI agents are becoming digital employees.

        Not because they're replacing humans.

        Because they're changing how work gets done.

        For decades, companies scaled by hiring more people.

        Now they're starting to scale by deploying more intelligence.

        An AI agent can:

        • Work 24/7
        • Use multiple tools
        • Analyze data
        • Execute workflows
        • Learn from feedback
        • Handle thousands of tasks simultaneously

        The most successful companies won't be the ones with the most employees.

        They'll be the ones that build the best collaboration between humans and AI.

        Humans will provide:

        🎯 Vision
        🧠 Judgment
        🤝 Relationships
        🚀 Innovation

        AI agents will provide:

        ⚡ Speed
        📈 Scale
        🔄 Automation
        🎯 Execution

        The future of work isn't Human vs AI.

        It's Human + AI.

        The question isn't:

        "Will AI agents replace jobs?"

        The real question is:

        "What tasks should humans never be doing in the first place?"

        What's one task in your work that an AI agent could handle today?

        👇 Curious to hear your thoughts.

        4h • Edited •

        Most businesses don't have a technology problem.

        They have an execution problem.

        Every week, we speak with companies looking for:

        🔹 A custom SaaS platform
        🔹 A mobile app
        🔹 An AI solution
        🔹 A Shopify store

        But after a few discussions, a different picture emerges.

        The real challenge is often:

        • Manual processes
        • Disconnected systems
        • Duplicate work
        • Operational bottlenecks

        Technology should solve business problems , Not create new ones.

        Before investing in your next software project, ask:

        ❓ What process are we trying to improve?
        ❓ How much is the current problem costing us?
        ❓ Can automation or integration solve it faster?

        The companies growing fastest today aren't necessarily building more software.

        They're removing friction, improving execution, and making smarter technology decisions.

        What's the biggest operational challenge your business is facing right now?

        Feed post

        Akshay Talekar

        • 3rd+

        Lead Salesforce QA Analyst| Accelq Automation Certified| Ex-Infocian| Experienced Salesforce/MSD365 QA Engineer | 7+ Years | Driving Quality across Sales & Service Clouds

        7h •

        When reCAPTCHA Blocks Automation 🤖

        Recently, I encountered a scenario where an AI-powered automation tool failed during form submission because Google reCAPTCHA detected the automated interaction and blocked the request.

        The Challenge

        * Form submission failed consistently
        * Regression tests became unstable
        * False defects were reported

        Root Cause
        reCAPTCHA is designed to prevent automated submissions, so the issue was not with ACCELQ but with the security mechanism itself.

        Solution
        ✅ Disable reCAPTCHA in QA/UAT environments for automation users
        ✅ Use Google test keys for automation
        ✅ Configure environment-based bypass for automated executions like

        IF $Environment = "QA"

        Log "reCAPTCHA Disabled for Automation"

        ELSE

        Log "Production Environment - reCAPTCHA Enabled"

        ENDIF

        Key Learning
        The objective of automation is to validate business functionality, not to break through security controls. A test-friendly architecture ensures stable automation while maintaining production security.

        #ACCELQ #AutomationTesting #QualityEngineering #Salesforce #SoftwareTesting #TestAutomation #ContinuousTesting #QE #AIInTesting #DevOps

        3 reactions3

        Most businesses think they have a lead generation problem.
        In reality, they have a follow-up problem.
        A lead visits your website.
        A lead fills out your form.
        A lead sends you a message.
        And then...
        ❌ The reply comes too late
        ❌ The follow-up never happens
        ❌ The prospect loses interest
        ❌ The sale goes to a competitor
        The truth is simple:
        The money is in the follow-up.
        Businesses that consistently follow up generate more sales, build more trust, and close more deals.
        That's why AI Automation is so powerful.
        Imagine if every lead received:
        ✅ An instant response
        ✅ Automated follow-ups
        ✅ Personalized nurturing

        ✅ CRM updates automatically

        Feed post

        Nikhil Anand

        • 3rd+

        DevOps Engineer at UST Global Services

        4h •

        Connect

        🚀 AI is no longer a "future skill" — it's becoming a core skill.
        A year ago, many of us were using AI mostly for quick searches, content generation, or code suggestions. Today, AI is helping teams automate workflows, improve productivity, accelerate development, and unlock knowledge that was previously buried in documents and systems.
        Recently, I worked on a Support Agent POC that leveraged LLMs and document retrieval capabilities. The idea was simple:
        📚 Collect project documents and knowledge assets
        🔍 Retrieve relevant information based on user queries
        🤖 Generate contextual answers through natural language interactions
        Instead of spending time searching through multiple documents, users can ask questions and receive answers grounded in project-specific knowledge.
        The most interesting lesson wasn't the technology itself—it was realizing that the quality of AI outcomes depends heavily on data, context, and how we interact with these systems.
        Some areas where I see AI creating immediate value:
        ✅ Knowledge Management
        ✅ Support Automation
        ✅ Incident Analysis
        ✅ Documentation Assistance
        ✅ Development Productivity
        ✅ Learning & Onboarding
        For professionals in every domain—not just software engineering—understanding AI is becoming as important as understanding the internet was years ago.
        You don't need to become an AI researcher.
        But learning concepts like LLMs, prompt engineering, RAG, agents, automation workflows, and responsible AI usage will increasingly become part of our daily work.
        The biggest risk today may not be AI replacing people.
        The bigger risk is people who learn AI outperforming people who don't.
        And yes... before anyone asks 😄
        I used AI while building an AI Support Agent, and I'm using AI to help refine this post about building AI. Looks like the machines have already started contributing to their own adoption.
        #AI #GenerativeAI #LLM #RAG #AIAgents #PromptEngineering #Automation #KnowledgeManagement #DevOps #SoftwareEngineering #Innovation #FutureOfWork

          Feed post

          Harsh Saxena

          • 3rd+

          Computer Science Specialist | AI/ML & Cognitive Product Implementation | NLP & Data Engineering | Cybersecurity & VAPT

          4h •

          Beyond the Prompt Why Enterprise AI is Moving from Chatbots to Autonomous Agents
          Most people view Generative AI as a conversational tool where you just type a prompt, you get text. But in enterprise environments like SaaS and automated procurement, a simple chat interface isn't enough. The future belongs to Agentic AI.
          During my time working on NLP architectures and Sentiment Analysis engines, I realized the real bottleneck isn't parsing the text it's execution.
          When an enterprise AI handles multi-structured data (like vendor agreements or contracts), it can't just summarize; it needs to:
          1️⃣ Extract core entities using advanced NLP.
          2️⃣ Cross-reference compliance metrics dynamically.
          3️⃣ Flag operational or security vulnerabilities (VAPT mindset).
          True cognitive automation doesn't just answer questions; it drives end-to-end delivery pipelines autonomously.

          What do you think is the biggest hurdle for enterprise AI agents today? Data privacy, model latency, or system integration?
          Let's discuss in the comments!

          #ArtificialIntelligence #GenerativeAI #NLP #DataScience #EnterpriseSaaS #AgenticAI

          23h •

          🤖 AI Is Creating a New Era for Marketing Operations

          For years, Marketing Operations teams have been responsible for:

          • Managing lead lifecycles
          • Building lead scoring models
          • Monitoring data quality
          • Creating reports and dashboards
          • Maintaining CRM and Marketing Automation platforms

          Now AI is becoming part of the operating model.

          Instead of spending hours finding problems, teams can focus on solving them.

          AI can help:

          ✅ Detect lead routing failures
          ✅ Identify duplicate records automatically
          ✅ Surface data quality issues in real time
          ✅ Predict conversion likelihood
          ✅ Recommend campaign optimizations
          ✅ Generate actionable insights from complex datasets

          The real value of AI is not writing emails.

          The real value is helping Marketing Operations teams make faster, smarter decisions using data.

          The organizations that gain the most from AI will be the ones that combine:

          • Clean data
          • Strong processes
          • Connected systems
          • Human expertise

          AI is powerful.

          But AI powered by reliable data is transformational.

          How is your Marketing Operations team using AI today?

          21h •

          🚀 One Decision Changed My Career in Software Testing

          When I started my QA journey, I had two choices:

          1️⃣ Stay comfortable with what my project required.

          2️⃣ Continuously learn skills beyond my project needs.

          I chose the second option.

          Over the years, I invested my time in learning:

          ✅ Automation Testing

          ✅ Selenium WebDriver

          ✅ API Automation

          ✅ Rest Assured

          ✅ Test Framework Design

          ✅ CI/CD Integration

          ✅ Mobile Automation Testing

          ✅ Quality Engineering Practices

          ✅ AI-Assisted Testing

          ✅ Tosca Automation

          What I realized is that career growth in QA is rarely about experience alone.

          It's about continuously expanding your capabilities and solving bigger problems.

          The most valuable lesson I've learned:

          👉 Don't limit yourself to a single tool.

          Learn the complete quality engineering ecosystem.

          A strong Automation Engineer understands:

          📌 Testing Fundamentals

          📌 Programming

          📌 APIs & Microservices

          📌 Automation Frameworks

          📌 CI/CD

          📌 Quality Engineering

          📌 AI-Powered Testing

          Technology will continue to evolve.

          The ability to learn and adapt will always remain your biggest advantage.

          Over the coming weeks, I'll be sharing practical insights on:

          🔹 Automation Testing

          🔹 Selenium

          🔹 Rest Assured

          🔹 Framework Development

          🔹 Quality Engineering

          🔹 Resume Building

          🔹 Interview Preparation

          🔹 Career Growth Strategies

          🔹 AI in Testing

          For fellow QA Engineers, Automation Testers, SDETs, and Quality Engineers:

          Feed post

          Gunjan Bisani

          • 3rd+

          Most businesses are stuck on memory, chasing, and manual follow-up. Building Oonh to fix that. Founder × 3. Bootstrapped.

          1h •

          Last week OONH Autopilot did something I didn't expect: it gave up on a lead. On purpose.

          Lead #82 had been getting polite, automated follow-ups. Eleven of them. On the twelfth, a cooldown rule I'd written months ago kicked in — if you've tried this many times and nothing's moving, stop and reassess.

          Instead of firing reminder #12, it paused, re-scored the whole history, and marked the lead disqualified at 95% confidence. The exact call I'd have made myself, if I'd had the time to look.

          I've spent most of my build time teaching these systems when to act. The harder problem, it turns out, is teaching them when to stop. An automation that never stops is just spam with extra steps. The restraint is the product.

          That was the moment OONH stopped feeling like software and started feeling like a teammate who knows when to back off.

          If you're building anything with AI agents — how are you handling the "when to stop" problem? I'm finding it much harder than "when to act."

          #BuildInPublic #AIagents #SaaS

          2 reactions2

          Send

          Feed post

          BIKASH BISWAL

          • 3rd+

          VP, Wells Fargo | AI Governance | AI Risk Management | Responsible AI | Enterprise Controls | Technology Risk | Data-Driven Risk Transformation | 11+ Years BFSI

          5h • Edited •

          AI is increasingly becoming a participant in how decisions are made.
          Recently, Morgan Stanley announced that AI agents can directly interact with parts of its wealth management platform, allowing AI systems to access information and perform tasks that traditionally required human interaction.
          That got me thinking.

          For years, organizations have designed operating models around three elements:

          People.
          Processes.
          Technology.
          AI Agents introduce a fourth.
          Autonomous participants.

          Unlike traditional automation, AI agents can:
          • Gather information
          • Reason across multiple inputs
          • Recommend actions
          • Trigger downstream processes
          • Interact with other systems

          The leadership challenge is no longer:
          "How do we automate work?"
          It is increasingly:
          "How do we manage a workforce that includes both humans and AI agents?"
          The organizations that will succeed may not be those with the most AI.

          They may be the ones that answer a few simple questions first:
          🔹 Which decisions should always remain human?
          🔹 Which decisions can be delegated?
          🔹 Who is accountable when an AI agent makes a mistake?
          🔹 How do we supervise digital workers at scale?

          For decades, management was about leading people.
          The next chapter may be about leading systems that can act.
          And that is a very different leadership challenge.
          What do you think will be the biggest challenge as AI agents become part of everyday business operations?

          #ArtificialIntelligence #AgenticAI #Leadership #FutureOfWork #FinancialServices #TechnologyTransformation #Innovation

          Feed post

          Anand B

          • 3rd+

          Regional Head – Operations | 18+ Yrs | P&L & EBITDA Leader | Multi-Unit | Food Service • Hospitality • Retail • Salon

          4h •

          A generation ago, this was technology.

          The PCO connected people across the world.
          The photo studio captured life’s important moments.

          Today, both fit inside a smartphone.

          What replaced them wasn’t a better phone booth or a better photo studio.

          It was a completely different way of solving the customer’s problem.

          That’s the lesson AI is teaching us today.

          Many businesses are asking:
          “How do we use AI?”

          A better question might be:
          “If AI changes how customers solve their problems, what part of our business model becomes irrelevant?”

          History shows that disruption rarely arrives as an upgrade.

          It arrives as a new way of creating value.

          PCO ➜ Smartphone

          Search Engine ➜ AI Assistant

          Manual Workflows ➜ Intelligent Automation

          The winners won’t be the organizations that resist change.

          They will be the ones that learn, adapt, and reinvent themselves before they are forced to.

          The future doesn’t arrive suddenly. It starts as something we underestimate.

          #AI #Leadership #DigitalTransformation #Innovation #FutureOfWork #BusinessStrategy

          We’re hiring — Qualitest Group (India)

          1. Automation Architect
          • 18+ yrs IT, 13+ yrs in QE/automation; proven leader in large-scale QE transformations.
          • Hands-on with UI/API/Mobile automation, AI-for-QE, agentic AI, cloud assurance (AWS/Azure/GCP/OCI).

          2. AI Developer (Proactive)
          • Strong Java/Python; work on GenAI solutions—prompt engineering, RAG, agentic flows, MCP integrations.
          • Experience with LLMs/APIs (OpenAI/Anthropic), vector DBs (FAISS/Pinecone/Weaviate), and scalable backends.
          • Familiar with microservices, REST APIs, CI/CD, and cloud platforms (AWS/Azure/GCP).

          • 5–10+ yrs in SDET/automation; strong in Playwright + TypeScript, Java, and DB testing.
          • CI/CD integration (GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps), cloud testing (Azure/AWS), and automation framework ownership.
          • Experience in reporting integrations, tooling POCs, and leading QA delivery in Agile teams.

          4. Quality Engineering — Team Lead (Automation)
          • 6+ yrs QA/automation leadership; define automation strategy, governance, and drive shift-left testing.
          • Hands-on with Cypress and JavaScript, API automation, framework design, CI/CD, and Agile/DevOps practices.

          Aniket Verma

          • 3rd+

          Healthcare & Technology Content Creator | Exploring AI, Digital Health & Emerging Innovations

          4h •

          A few years ago, booking a doctor’s appointment often meant long phone calls, waiting on hold, and unnecessary delays.

          Today, AI is completely changing that experience.

          Patients can now:

          ✅ Schedule appointments in minutes
          ✅ Receive automated reminders
          ✅ Get faster responses to common questions
          ✅ Access personalised health recommendations
          ✅ Experience shorter wait times and smoother care journeys

          But the real value of AI in healthcare isn't just automation.

          It's creating a better patient experience.

          When healthcare professionals spend less time on administrative tasks, they can focus more on what matters most: patient care.

          The healthcare organizations that successfully combine technology with human expertise will be the ones that build stronger patient trust and deliver better outcomes.

          AI isn't replacing healthcare professionals.

          It's helping them provide care more efficiently, accurately, and personally.

          The future of healthcare isn't just digital.

          It's patient-centered, data-driven, and powered by intelligent technology.

          How do you think AI will have the biggest impact on patient experience over the next five years?

          #Healthcare #AI #HealthTech #DigitalHealth #PatientExperience #HealthcareInnovation #ArtificialIntelligence #FutureOfHealthcare

          Feed post

          Mayur Chaudhari

          • 3rd+

          Full Stack Engineer | Node.js | React.js | Redux | Redis | Scalable Systems | AI-powered SaaS

          6h •

          🔹 The future of freelance work is not about banning AI, but about understanding the nuances of automation. As we delve into the world of AI-powered proposal drafting, it's essential to recognize that the core technical friction lies in the distinction between automation and augmentation. The key challenge is not the use of AI itself, but rather the level of human involvement in the decision-making process. Upwork's policy, for instance, explicitly prohibits tools that auto-submit proposals, scrape job feeds, or make background requests, while allowing AI-powered drafting tools that require human review and submission.

          The mechanics of this distinction are crucial, as they determine the legitimacy of AI usage in freelance work. Implementation hurdles arise when tools attempt to blur the line between automation and augmentation, often resulting in unintended consequences. The data flow in such cases can become convoluted, leading to inconsistencies and potential account suspensions. A deeper examination of the algorithmic nuances reveals that the crux of the issue lies in the agency of the submit button – who clicks it, and under what circumstances.

          Some key insights to consider:
          🔹 Performance: AI-powered drafting tools can significantly enhance proposal quality and efficiency, but only when used in conjunction with human oversight.
          🔹 Scalability: The use of AI in freelance work can be scaled up or down depending on the specific needs of the project, as long as the human element remains intact.
          🔹 Gotcha/Trade-off: The primary trade-off lies in the level of control and agency – automated tools may offer speed and convenience, but at the risk of account suspension and lost revenue.

          In conclusion, the production-readiness verdict for AI-powered proposal drafting tools is contingent upon their ability to maintain human involvement in the decision-making process. As we move forward, it's essential to prioritize the development of tools that augment human capabilities, rather than attempting to replace them entirely. What will be the breaking point for AI-powered freelance work at scale – will it be the development of more sophisticated automation tools, or the emergence of new platforms that prioritize human agency? #AIinFreelance #FutureOfWork #FreelanceAutomation #UpworkPolicy #AIAugmentation #HumanCenteredDesign #FreelanceInnovation #TechForFreelancers #FreelanceScaling #ArtificialIntelligenceInBusiness
